body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Codex 0.130.0 ships a genuine Claude-Code-style **Plan Mode** â€” a *collaboration mode*, not a command-approval policy. It is entered interactively with the `/plan` slash command (present in the native binary's slash registry alongside `/clear`, `/compact`, `/model`, `/status`). Once in Plan Mode the session carries `collaboration_mode.mode = "plan"` (visible on `turn_context` / `task_started.collaboration_mode_kind`) and a dedicated developer prompt titled `# Plan Mode (Conversational)`: "You are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it... You must not perform **mutating** actions... When you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially." The prompt is explicit that the plan is a review gate: "The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block... Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan." That is a plan-drafts-then-blocks-on-user-decision gate â€” distinct from codex's `--ask-for-approval` command-approval policy (a per-command sandbox prompt, NOT plan mode).

This is observed empirically, not just in docs: two real local rollouts captured under codex 0.130.0 (`019e1d3e-â€¦` 2026-05-12 and `019e2c55-â€¦` 2026-05-15, both `originator: codex-tui`) contain assistant `final_answer` messages wrapping a real `<proposed_plan>â€¦</proposed_plan>` block, immediately followed by `task_complete` with the turn ending and no further agent activity until the user replies.

## How irrlicht observes it

The Source variant is `FilesUnderRoot{~/.codex/sessions, JSONLineParser}`. The codex parser (parser.go `parseCodexMessage`, lines 192â€“199) detects a *complete* `<proposed_plan>â€¦</proposed_plan>` span inside an **assistant** `text`/`output_text` content block (role-gated: a developer-prompt mention does NOT fire â€” `assistantContentContainsBlock` + the developer-role guard) and synthesizes a tool_use `{ID: "codex-proposed-plan", Name: "ExitPlanMode"}`. That open tool call survives the subsequent `task_complete` (mapped to `turn_done`): the tailer carries `HasOpenToolCall = true` with `ExitPlanMode` in `LastOpenToolNames`, so the state classifier routes the session to **waiting** rather than ready even though the turn is "done." This is exactly the same waiting branch claudecode reaches via a real ExitPlanMode tool_use. It is verified by unit tests `TestParser_ProposedPlan_SynthesizesExitPlanMode`, `TestParser_ProposedPlan_EndToEndWaitingState` (HasOpenToolCall=true after proposed_plan + task_complete), `TestParser_ProposedPlan_DeveloperMessageDoesNotTrigger`, `TestParser_ProposedPlan_PartialTagDoesNotTrigger`, and the dedupe/close-by-user-reply tests, plus the committed regression fixture `replaydata/agents/codex/regression/proposed-plan-pending/`. On the user's next prompt the synthetic ExitPlanMode is cleared (`ClearToolNames` on the user message) so the session recovers waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready â€” the full approval arc.

Observability caveat: codex Plan Mode is **TUI-only**. There is no `codex exec` (headless) flag and no `-c` config key to start a session in plan mode (confirmed against `codex --help`, `codex exec --help`, and the feature-flag registry â€” `collaboration_modes` shows as a removed flag, but the `/plan` runtime command is live). The recipe therefore drives the interactive REPL (`drive-codex-interactive.sh`, `codex --no-alt-screen`), entering plan mode via the `/plan` slash step before prompting. Despite the scenario's `requires: [headless_mode]` tag, the *observable lifecycle* (workingâ†’waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready on one live rollout) is identical to the claudecode arc; the only difference is the entry channel (interactive `/plan` vs claudecode's headless `permissions.defaultMode=plan`).

## Capability key

Primitive exercised = `plan_mode`. codex/capabilities.json currently marks `plan_mode: "unknown"`; this assessment supports flipping it to `"yes"` (the parent updates capabilities.json centrally).
caveats: Plan Mode is TUI-only â€” there is no headless `codex exec` entry point and no `-c collaboration_mode=plan` config key (codex 0.130.0). The recipe must drive the interactive REPL and enter via the `/plan` slash command, unlike claudecode's headless `permissions.defaultMode=plan`.

The `/plan` slash toggles a mode and does NOT produce a turn (no task_complete), so the recipe uses a sleep â€” not wait_turn â€” after the slash; wait_turn would over-count against the EXPECTED_TURNS bump that every send/slash step applies.

Plan Mode's developer prompt encourages a PHASE-1 exploration/clarifying-question pass before emitting the plan; on a real repo the first proposed_plan can be many minutes out. The recipe runs in an empty fresh cwd and prompts for an immediate single-turn `<proposed_plan>` to keep the turn short and deterministic.

The waiting state is driven by a SYNTHETIC ExitPlanMode (codex has no structured ExitPlanMode tool_use); it is the `<proposed_plan>` text span the parser keys on, so a malformed or unclosed tag would not gate.

sources.note: parseCodexMessage lines 192-199 synthesize ExitPlanMode from a complete <proposed_plan>â€¦</proposed_plan> assistant block; assistantContentContainsBlock + developer-role guard
TestParser_ProposedPlan_EndToEndWaitingState â€” HasOpenToolCall=true with ExitPlanMode after proposed_plan + task_complete; plus Synthesize/DeveloperMessage/PartialTag/Dedupe/ClosedByUserReply tests
Committed regression fixture derived from a real codex plan-mode rollout: collaboration_mode.mode=plan, assistant final_answer with <proposed_plan>, then task_complete

Real codex 0.130.0 TUI rollout â€” turn_context collaboration_mode.mode=plan with the full '# Plan Mode (Conversational)' developer prompt; assistant final_answer emits a genuine <proposed_plan> block followed by task_complete
user-blocking-plan-mode-approval Primitive exercised names 'Codex <proposed_plan> block' as a valid plan_mode anchor
Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.codex/sessions, JSONLineParser} â€” the transcript-tailer path the synthetic ExitPlanMode flows through
OpenAI Codex CLI â€” Plan Mode is a collaboration mode (verified against codex-cli 0.130.0 binary slash registry: /plan present)
